<!-- Generated by spec-lite v0.0.8 | sub-agent: planner | date: 2026-04-14 -->

# Plan: WebSocket & SSE Protocol Support

## 1. Overview

Add WebSocket (WS) and Server-Sent Events (SSE) protocol support to Wave Client as first-class request types alongside existing HTTP requests. Users select a protocol via a stylized dropdown in the request editor; the UI morphs to match the protocol's interaction model — bidirectional chat for WS, unidirectional event stream for SSE. New backend services (`WebSocketService`, `SseService`) manage connections server-side, following the same architecture as `HttpService`. WS/SSE items live in collections but are excluded from Run Collection, Flows, and Test Suites. Both VS Code and Web platforms are supported; MCP support is out of scope.

**Target users:** API developers testing WebSocket and SSE endpoints alongside traditional REST APIs.

**Decomposition strategy:** Vertical slicing — each feature delivers testable, end-to-end value. Implementation order maximizes early feedback and minimizes integration risk.

### Dependency Graph & Recommended Implementation Order

```
FEAT-001 (Types) ─────┬─── FEAT-002 (State) ─── FEAT-003 (UI Shell) ─┬─ FEAT-006 (WS UI)
                       │                                                ├─ FEAT-007 (SSE UI)

                       └─── FEAT-011 (Exclusions)
```

**Recommended order:** FEAT-001 → FEAT-002 → FEAT-003 → FEAT-011 → FEAT-004 & FEAT-005 (parallel) → FEAT-006 & FEAT-007 (parallel) → FEAT-008 & FEAT-009 (parallel) → FEAT-010

## 3. Tech Stack Additions

> The canonical tech stack is defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Tech Stack.
> Only **additions or overrides** specific to this plan are listed here.

| Component          | Technology               | Justification                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| WebSocket client   | `ws` ^8                  | Node.js WebSocket library — lightweight, standards-compliant, supports custom headers on upgrade request (unlike the browser `WebSocket` API). Used server-side in `packages/shared`. MIT licensed.                                         |
| SSE frame parsing  | Custom over `axios` stream | `axios` is already in the stack. Using `responseType: 'stream'` gives raw SSE frames. Custom parser handles `event:`, `data:`, `id:`, `retry:` fields. Avoids adding a separate `eventsource` library; also needed for POST-based SSE support + custom headers which standard EventSource API doesn't support. |

No other tech stack changes. All UI uses existing React + Tailwind + Radix + Lucide stack.

## 4. Data Model (High-Level)

### Domain Concepts

- **CollectionRequest (HTTP)** — Existing type, unchanged. Gains optional `protocol?: 'http'` field for explicit protocol identification. Absent `protocol` defaults to `'http'` on load (backward compatibility).
- **WsCollectionRequest** — New type for WebSocket requests. Contains URL (auto-detected scheme: `ws://` or `wss://`), optional headers and query parameters. No HTTP method (always WS upgrade), no request body, no response validation. `protocol: 'ws'` discriminant.
- **SseCollectionRequest** — New type for SSE requests. Contains HTTP method (GET, POST, etc. — POST-based SSE supported), URL, headers, query params, and optional request body. No response validation (streaming, no single response to validate). `protocol: 'sse'` discriminant.
- **AnyCollectionRequest** — Discriminated union: `CollectionRequest | WsCollectionRequest | SseCollectionRequest`. This is the type used by `CollectionItem.request`.
- **RealtimeConnection** — Ephemeral runtime state (not persisted). Contains: connection ID, connection status, response headers (from WS upgrade or SSE initial response), messages/events array. Scoped to a tab; cleared on disconnect or tab close.
- **WsMessage** — A single WebSocket message. Direction (`sent` | `received`), content (text only for MVP), timestamp, size in bytes.
- **SseEvent** — A single SSE event. Event name (from `event:` field, defaults to `'message'`), data (from `data:` field), event ID (from `id:` field), timestamp.
- **ConnectionStatus** — Enum: `'idle'` | `'connecting'` | `'connected'` | `'disconnecting'` | `'disconnected'` | `'error'`.

### Type Hierarchy

```typescript
// Protocol discriminant
type RequestProtocol = 'http' | 'ws' | 'sse';

// Existing HTTP request — gains optional protocol field
interface CollectionRequest {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    protocol?: 'http';       // Optional for backward compat
    method: string;           // GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS
    url: CollectionUrl | string;
    query?: ParamRow[];
    header?: HeaderRow[];
    body?: CollectionBody;
    description?: string;
    validation?: RequestValidation;
    authId?: string;
    sourceRef?: CollectionReference;
}

// New WebSocket request
interface WsCollectionRequest {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    protocol: 'ws';
    url: CollectionUrl | string;  // ws:// or wss://
    header?: HeaderRow[];
    query?: ParamRow[];
    description?: string;
    authId?: string;
    sourceRef?: CollectionReference;
}

// New SSE request
interface SseCollectionRequest {
    id: string;
    name: string;
    protocol: 'sse';
    method: string;               // GET, POST, etc.
    url: CollectionUrl | string;
    header?: HeaderRow[];
    query?: ParamRow[];
    body?: CollectionBody;        // For POST-based SSE
    description?: string;
    authId?: string;
    sourceRef?: CollectionReference;
}

// Discriminated union
type AnyCollectionRequest = CollectionRequest | WsCollectionRequest | SseCollectionRequest;

// Type guards
function isHttpRequest(req: AnyCollectionRequest): req is CollectionRequest;
function isWsRequest(req: AnyCollectionRequest): req is WsCollectionRequest;
function isSseRequest(req: AnyCollectionRequest): req is SseCollectionRequest;
function getRequestProtocol(req: AnyCollectionRequest): RequestProtocol;
```

### Connection & Message Types

```typescript
// WebSocket message
interface WsMessage {
    id: string;
    direction: 'sent' | 'received';
    content: string;              // Text only (MVP)
    timestamp: number;
    size: number;                 // Bytes
}

// SSE event
interface SseEvent {
    id: string;
    eventName: string;            // From SSE `event:` field, defaults to 'message'
    data: string;                 // From SSE `data:` field
    eventId?: string;             // From SSE `id:` field
    timestamp: number;
}

// Connection status
type ConnectionStatus = 'idle' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnecting' | 'disconnected' | 'error';

// Per-tab realtime state
interface RealtimeConnectionState {
    connectionId: string | null;
    status: ConnectionStatus;
    responseHeaders: Record<string, string>;
    wsMessages: WsMessage[];      // For WS tabs
    sseEvents: SseEvent[];        // For SSE tabs
    error?: string;
    connectedAt?: number;
}
```

### Connection Configuration Types

```typescript
// WebSocket connection config
interface WsConnectionConfig {
    id: string;                   // Unique connection ID
    url: string;                  // ws:// or wss://
    headers?: Record<string, string>;
    params?: string;              // Query string
    auth?: HttpAuth;              // Reuse existing auth infrastructure
}

// SSE connection config
interface SseConnectionConfig {
    id: string;                   // Unique connection ID
    method: string;               // GET, POST, etc.
    url: string;
    headers?: Record<string, string>;
    params?: string;
    body?: unknown;               // For POST-based SSE
    auth?: HttpAuth;
}
```

### Adapter Handle Types (Event-Driven)

```typescript
// WebSocket connection handle (event-driven, like StreamHandle)
interface WsConnectionHandle {
    connectionId: string;
    onMessage(cb: (msg: WsMessage) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onStatusChange(cb: (status: ConnectionStatus) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onError(cb: (error: string) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onHeaders(cb: (headers: Record<string, string>) => void): Unsubscribe;
}

// SSE connection handle
interface SseConnectionHandle {
    connectionId: string;
    onEvent(cb: (event: SseEvent) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onStatusChange(cb: (status: ConnectionStatus) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onError(cb: (error: string) => void): Unsubscribe;
    onHeaders(cb: (headers: Record<string, string>) => void): Unsubscribe;
}

type Unsubscribe = () => void;
```

### Conceptual Relationships

- `CollectionItem` → `AnyCollectionRequest` (1:1) — A collection item holds an HTTP, WS, or SSE request definition
- `TabData` → `AnyCollectionRequest` + `RealtimeConnectionState` — Each open tab has the request config plus ephemeral connection state
- `RealtimeConnectionState` → `WsMessage[]` or `SseEvent[]` — Messages/events scoped to connection, in-memory only
- `Collection` → `CollectionItem[]` — WS/SSE items nest alongside HTTP items in existing collection tree structure

### Storage Strategy

- **Request definitions** — Stored in existing collection JSON files (`.waveclient/collections/`). WS/SSE items serialize with `protocol` discriminant field. No new storage format needed.
- **Runtime connection state** — In-memory only (Zustand store). No disk persistence for connections, messages, or events.
- **Backward compatibility** — Existing collections without `protocol` field default to `'http'` on deserialization. No migration needed.

## 5. Interface Design

### Protocol Selector UI

The `ProtocolSelector` dropdown appears in the request editor toolbar, before the Save button area. Each option has:
- **Visual icon** (Lucide): `Globe` for HTTP, `Cable` for WS, `Radio` for SSE
- **Stylized label** with protocol-specific color accent
- Changing protocol morphs the editor UI below

### Request Editor Layout per Protocol

#### HTTP (default — unchanged)
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Method ▼] [URL input                    ] [Send] [Save ▼] [Protocol ▼] │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Params] [Headers] [Body] [Validation]                       │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ Request content (tab-specific)                           │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Body] [Headers] [Cookies] [Validation]                      │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ Response content (tab-specific)                          │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### WebSocket
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [URL input (ws://...)               ] [● Connected] [Disconnect] [Save ▼] [Protocol ▼] │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Params] [Headers]                                            │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ Request config (tab-specific)                            │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Messages]  [Response Headers]                               │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ ↑ Received: {"status":"ok"}              14:32:01  128B │ │
│ │ ↓ Sent: {"subscribe":"users"}            14:32:00   24B │ │
│ │ ↑ Received: Connected                    14:31:59    9B │ │
│ ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │
│ │ [Message input                         ] [Send ▶]       │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key WS UI elements:**
- No HTTP method dropdown (always WS upgrade)
- URL input accepts `ws://` or `wss://` (auto-detected from scheme)
- Status indicator: colored dot + label (`Idle` / `Connecting...` / `Connected` / `Disconnected` / `Error`)
- Connect/Disconnect toggle button (replaces Send button)
- Messages tab: chat-like interface with direction arrows (↑ sent, ↓ received), timestamps, byte sizes
- Message composer at bottom with text input + Send button (disabled when not connected)
- Response Headers tab: headers captured from WS upgrade handshake

#### SSE
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Method ▼] [URL input               ] [● Connected] [Disconnect] [Save ▼] [Protocol ▼] │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Params] [Headers] [Body]                                    │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ Request config (tab-specific, includes body for POST)    │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ [Events] [Response Headers]      [Filter: All Events ▼]     │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ ● update   {"users":["alice","bob"]}     14:32:05       │ │
│ │ ● message  {"heartbeat":true}            14:32:00       │ │
│ │ ● connect  {"status":"ready"}            14:31:59       │ │
│ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key SSE UI elements:**
- HTTP method dropdown (GET, POST, etc. — POST-based SSE supported)
- URL input (standard http:// or https://)
- Status indicator + Connect/Disconnect (replaces Send button)
- Body tab present (for POST-based SSE endpoints)
- Events tab: chronological event list with event name badge, data preview, timestamp
- Event name filter dropdown: lists unique event names received, allows filtering to one or "All Events"
- Response Headers tab: headers from initial HTTP response
- No message composer (SSE is server→client only)

### Adapter Interface Additions

```typescript
// Added to IPlatformAdapter
interface IRealtimeAdapter {
    // WebSocket
    connectWebSocket(config: WsConnectionConfig): WsConnectionHandle;
    disconnectWebSocket(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>;
    sendWebSocketMessage(connectionId: string, message: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>;

    // SSE
    connectSse(config: SseConnectionConfig): SseConnectionHandle;
    disconnectSse(connectionId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>;
}
```

### Server Routes (Web Platform)

| Route                  | Method | Body                      | Response                  | Purpose                          |
| ---------------------- | ------ | ------------------------- | ------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| `/api/ws/connect`      | POST   | `WsConnectionConfig`      | `{ connectionId }`        | Open WS connection on server     |
| `/api/ws/disconnect`   | POST   | `{ connectionId }`        | `void`                    | Close WS connection              |
| `/api/ws/send`         | POST   | `{ connectionId, message }`| `void`                   | Send text message over WS        |
| `/api/sse/connect`     | POST   | `SseConnectionConfig`     | `{ connectionId }`        | Open SSE stream on server        |
| `/api/sse/disconnect`  | POST   | `{ connectionId }`        | `void`                    | Close SSE stream                 |

Server-to-browser event relay uses the existing WebSocket push channel (same infrastructure as `emitStateChange`) with new event types:
- `ws.message` — WS message received
- `ws.status` — WS connection status change
- `ws.headers` — WS upgrade headers
- `ws.error` — WS connection error
- `sse.event` — SSE event received
- `sse.status` — SSE connection status change
- `sse.headers` — SSE response headers
- `sse.error` — SSE connection error

## 6. Security Considerations

> Standing security rules are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Security.

### Plan-Specific Security Concerns

- **WS URL validation** — Validate that WS URLs use `ws://` or `wss://` scheme before connecting. Reject `javascript:`, `data:`, and other dangerous schemes. Apply the same URL validation as HTTP requests.
- **Auth on WS/SSE** — Reuse existing auth infrastructure (`HttpAuth`). Auth headers are applied to the WS upgrade request and SSE initial request. Bearer tokens, API keys, and Basic auth work naturally via headers. Digest and NTLM auth are not applicable to WS/SSE (silently ignored or show warning).
- **Connection limits** — Enforce maximum concurrent connections per tab and globally to prevent resource exhaustion. Suggested limits: 1 active connection per tab, 10 globally.
- **Message size limits** — Cap WS message display at a reasonable size (e.g., 1MB) to prevent UI freeze from very large messages. Truncate with "message too large" indicator.
- **Proxy/TLS** — WS/SSE connections should respect the same proxy and certificate settings as HTTP requests. `wss://` inherits TLS/cert validation settings.
- **No credential leakage** — Ensure WS/SSE connection configs with auth tokens are never logged in full. Follow existing logging rules from memory.

## 7. Architecture & Design (Plan-Specific)

> Standing architecture principles are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Architecture.

### Plan-Specific Decisions

- **Event-driven connection handles** — WS/SSE connections use callback-based handles (`WsConnectionHandle`, `SseConnectionHandle`) rather than Promises, following the same pattern as Arena's `StreamHandle`. This is the right fit for long-lived, event-emitting connections where Promise-based APIs would be awkward.
- **Ephemeral message state** — WS/SSE messages live only in Zustand store (in-memory). No persistence to disk or history. Rationale: WS/SSE sessions can produce thousands of messages; persisting them would bloat storage and complicate the data model. Users can copy individual messages if needed.
- **Service-per-protocol in packages/shared** — `WebSocketService` and `SseService` are separate service classes (not a single `RealtimeService`) because their connection models are fundamentally different (bidirectional vs unidirectional, upgrade handshake vs HTTP stream). Keeping them separate follows the single-responsibility principle and makes each service independently testable.
- **Server-side connection management** — All WS/SSE connections are established server-side (in the extension host for VS Code, in packages/server for web). The UI never connects directly. This is consistent with the existing architecture where `HttpService` runs server-side and maintains platform independence.
- **Protocol selector location** — The protocol dropdown is in the editor toolbar, separate from the HTTP method dropdown. This avoids conflating protocol choice with HTTP verb selection, and makes it clear that WS has no method concept.
- **Unified connection controls component** — The `ConnectionControls` component (status indicator + connect/disconnect) is shared between WS and SSE UIs. Only the downstream content (Messages vs Events) differs.
- **Manual reconnect only** — No auto-reconnect with backoff. Users click Connect when they want to reconnect. Simpler, more predictable, and avoids invisible retry loops that could mask server issues.

## 8. Coding Standards (Plan-Specific Overrides)

> Standing coding standards are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Coding Standards.

No overrides — see memory.

## 9. Testing Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing testing conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Testing.

### Plan-Specific Testing Requirements

- **WebSocketService unit tests** — Mock `ws` library. Test connection lifecycle (open, close, error), message send/receive, upgrade header capture, proxy configuration, timeout handling.
- **SseService unit tests** — Mock axios stream. Test SSE frame parsing (single-line data, multi-line data, named events, event ID, retry field, empty lines, comments). Test connection lifecycle.
- **Protocol selector component tests** — Verify protocol switching morphs the editor UI. Verify HTTP path is completely unchanged when HTTP is selected.
- **WS UI component tests** — Test connection controls (connect/disconnect button states), message list rendering, message composer (send on Enter, disabled when disconnected), status indicator states.
- **SSE UI component tests** — Test event stream rendering, event name filter/group behavior, connect/disconnect flow.
- **Exclusion gate tests** — Verify WS/SSE items are filtered from RunCollectionModal, FlowRequestSearch, and TestSuiteRequestSearch.
- **Type guard tests** — Verify `isHttpRequest()`, `isWsRequest()`, `isSseRequest()` correctly discriminate all request types including backward-compat (missing `protocol` → HTTP).
- **Integration tests for adapters** — Test VS Code adapter postMessage relay and Web adapter REST+WebSocket relay for WS/SSE operations via mock backends.
- **Backward compatibility tests** — Load existing collection files (no `protocol` field). Verify all requests are treated as HTTP. Verify save/load round-trip preserves `protocol` field.

## 10. Logging Strategy (Plan-Specific)

> Standing logging conventions are defined in `.spec-lite/memory.md` → Logging.

### Plan-Specific Logging Requirements

- **Connection lifecycle events** — Log connection attempts, successful connections, disconnections, and errors for both WS and SSE with connection ID and URL (no auth tokens).
- **Message counts** — Log message/event counts periodically (not individual messages — could be high volume). E.g., "WS connection abc123: 42 messages sent, 156 received in last 60s."
- **SSE parse errors** — Log malformed SSE frames with frame content (truncated to 200 chars) for debugging.
- **Connection timeouts** — Log when connections are closed due to inactivity or server-side timeout.
